dashboard.description: Manage your code reviews and AI-powered PR analysis from one place.
dashboard.gettingStartedDesc: To begin using CoReview, follow these steps:
dashboard.step1: Connect a Git provider (GitHub, GitLab, etc.)
dashboard.step2: Add repositories you want to track
dashboard.step3: Configure an LLM server for AI-powered reviews
dashboard.step4: Set up review contexts to customize analysis

gitProviders.title: Git Providers
gitProviders.description: Manage your git repository providers and their configurations.
gitProviders.apiError: Failed to connect to PR-Agent API
gitProviders.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.

empty.title: No Git Providers
empty.description: Get started by adding your first git provider.

emptyState.title: No Git Providers
emptyState.description: Get started by adding your first git provider to connect your repositories.

webhooksTab.title: Repository webhooks
webhooksTab.description: Register and manage per-repository webhooks for this provider.
webhooksTab.appModeBanner: This GitHub provider is configured as a GitHub App. App installations deliver webhooks to the App's own URL, so per-repository webhooks are not needed here. Manage App events in the GitHub App settings instead.
webhooksTab.unsupportedProvider: UI-managed webhook registration is not yet available for this provider type. Any existing rows are listed for reference.

newProvider.title: Add Git Provider
newProvider.description: Connect a new git provider to start tracking repositories.

editProvider.title: Edit Git Provider
editProvider.description: Update the configuration for this git provider.

form.name: Display Name
form.nameHelp: A friendly name to identify this provider.
form.baseUrlHelp: The base URL for self-hosted instances. Leave empty for cloud-hosted services.
form.accessTokenPlaceholderEdit: Leave empty to keep current token
form.accessTokenHelp: A personal access token with repository access permissions.
form.webhookSecretHelp: Used to verify webhook payloads from the provider.
form.deploymentTypeHelp: Choose how to authenticate with GitHub.
form.deploymentTypeUserHelp: Use a personal access token for simple setups. Requires a token with 'repo' scope.
form.deploymentTypeAppHelp: Use a GitHub App for organization-wide access with fine-grained permissions.
form.userTokenHelp: A GitHub personal access token with 'repo' scope.
form.appIdHelp: The numeric ID of your GitHub App.
form.privateKeyPlaceholder:
-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
...
-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
form.privateKeyPlaceholderEdit: Leave empty to keep current private key
form.privateKeyHelp: The RSA private key generated for your GitHub App.
form.autoSyncOnCreate: Auto-sync with DevLake after creating provider
form.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.

devlake.title: DevLake Integration
devlake.description: Configure connection, scopes, and run ingestion for this provider.
devlake.enabled: Enable DevLake integration for this provider
devlake.invalidScopesJson: Scopes must be a valid JSON array.
devlake.syncFailed: Sync failed. Check backend logs and DevLake credentials.

deleteDialog.title: Delete Git Provider
deleteDialog.description: Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"? This will also delete all associated repositories and their review history. This action cannot be undone.

error.title: Something went wrong
error.description: Failed to load git providers. Please try again.

llmProviders.title: LLM Providers
llmProviders.description: Manage your LLM providers for AI-powered code reviews.
llmProviders.apiError: Failed to connect to PR-Agent API
llmProviders.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.

empty.title: No LLM Providers
empty.description: Get started by adding your first LLM provider.

newProvider.title: Add LLM Provider
newProvider.description: Connect a new LLM provider to power AI code reviews.

editProvider.title: Edit LLM Provider
editProvider.description: Update the configuration for this LLM provider.

form.typeCannotChange: Provider type cannot be changed after creation.
form.name: Display Name
form.nameHelp: A friendly name to identify this provider.
form.apiKeyPlaceholderEdit: Leave empty to keep current key
form.apiKeyHelp: Your provider's API key for authentication.
form.apiBaseHelp: Custom API endpoint. Leave empty for the default endpoint.
form.apiVersionHelp: The Azure OpenAI API version to use.
form.vertexProjectHelp: Your Google Cloud Platform project ID.
form.vertexLocationHelp: The GCP region for Vertex AI.
form.clientIdPlaceholder: Enter your Azure AD client ID
form.clientIdHelp: The client ID from your Azure AD app registration.
form.clientSecretPlaceholderEdit: Leave empty to keep current secret
form.clientSecretHelp: The client secret from your Azure AD app registration.
form.tenantIdPlaceholder: Enter your Azure AD tenant ID
form.tenantIdHelp: Your Azure Active Directory tenant ID.
form.awsAccessKeyIdPlaceholder: Enter your AWS access key ID
form.awsAccessKeyIdHelp: Your AWS access key ID for Bedrock.
form.awsSecretAccessKeyPlaceholder: Enter your AWS secret access key
form.awsSecretAccessKeyPlaceholderEdit: Leave empty to keep current key
form.awsSecretAccessKeyHelp: Your AWS secret access key for Bedrock.
form.modelIdHelp: The Bedrock model ID to use (optional).
form.fetchModelsFailed: Failed to fetch models. Please check your API key and try again.
form.modelsAutoFetch: Models will be fetched automatically when you enter a valid API key.
form.modelHelp: Enter a model name or fetch available models using your API key.
form.modelHelpFetched: Select from the available models fetched from your provider.
form.fallbackModelsHelp: Models to use if the primary model fails or is unavailable.
form.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.description: Engineering insights from Apache DevLake — powered by Grafana and filtered by your selected provider scopes.

notConfigured.title: Grafana dashboard not configured
notConfigured.description: Set the NEXT_PUBLIC_DEVLAKE_GRAFANA_URL environment variable to the embed URL of your Apache DevLake Grafana dashboard.

loadError.title: Unable to display the dashboard
loadError.description: Your browser blocked the embedded Grafana instance. Check that Grafana allows embedding (allow_embedding = true) and that the URL is correct.

learnings.title: Learnings
learnings.description: Knowledge the AI reviewer has captured from past PR discussions.

disabled.title: Knowledge base disabled
disabled.description: Enable KNOWLEDGE_BASE__ENABLED in your PR-Agent configuration to start capturing learnings.

empty.title: No learnings yet
empty.description: Learnings will appear here after reviewers reply to PR review comments with guidance for the AI.

status.raw_tooltip: Stored verbatim. The background worker will refine the wording before it is shown in reviews.
status.refined_tooltip: Ready to be applied in future reviews.
status.rejected_tooltip: Rejected during refinement. Review or delete this entry.

deleteDialog.title: Delete learning
deleteDialog.description: This will remove the learning from the knowledge base. This action cannot be undone.

learnings.apiError: Failed to connect to PR-Agent API

automation.title: Automation
automation.description: Control which PR-Agent commands run automatically when pull requests are opened or updated. Changes take effect on the next webhook delivery — no redeploy required.

global.title: Global switches
global.description: Settings applied across every git provider.
global.disableAutoFeedbackHelp: When enabled, PR-Agent will not run any automatic tools on new PRs or pushes. Comment triggers still work.

provider.title: Per-provider behavior
provider.description: Pick which commands run automatically on each supported git provider.

fields.prCommandsHelp: Run when a PR is opened, reopened, or marked ready for review. Add slash commands like /review, /describe, /improve.
fields.prCommandsEmpty: No commands will run automatically on new PRs.
fields.pushCommands: Commands on push to open PR
fields.pushCommandsHelp: Run when new commits are pushed to an open PR. Requires the push trigger to be enabled.
fields.pushCommandsEmpty: No commands will run on new pushes.
fields.handlePushTriggerHelp: Listen for commits pushed to open PRs and run the push commands above.
fields.handlePrActionsHelp: Which GitHub pull_request event types should invoke the PR commands.
fields.feedbackOnDraftHelp: By default PR-Agent skips draft PRs. Enable to include them.

actions.reloadHelp: Forces the webhook service to re-read config from the database on its next delivery.

messages.saved: Automation config saved. Webhook service will pick up changes on the next delivery.

tokenLimits.title: Token Limits
tokenLimits.description: Configure token limits for PR descriptions, commits, and model context windows.

form.maxDescriptionTokensHelp: Maximum number of tokens for PR description analysis.
form.maxCommitsTokensHelp: Maximum number of tokens for commit message analysis.
form.maxModelTokensHelp: Maximum context window size for the LLM model.
form.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.

webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.description: Register and manage per-repository webhooks on your git providers.
webhooks.newDescription: Create a draft webhook, then register it on the provider.
webhooks.editDescription: Update webhook configuration. Save changes before re-registering.
webhooks.deleteConfirm: Delete webhook for {repo}? This also removes it on the provider.

empty.title: No webhooks configured
empty.description: Register a webhook to start receiving events from a repository.

form.providerLockedNote: Provider cannot be changed after creation. Delete and recreate to switch.
form.repoHint: Format varies by provider: owner/repo (GitHub), group/project (GitLab).
form.eventsHint: Leave empty to use provider defaults. Click presets or add custom events.
form.eventsEmpty: No events set - provider defaults will be used.
form.secretHintCreate: Used by the provider to sign payloads. A random secret is generated if you leave this blank.
form.secretHintEdit: Enter a new value to rotate the secret. Leave blank to keep the current one.
form.activeHint: Inactive webhooks are stored but the provider will not deliver events.
form.insecureSslHint: Disables TLS certificate checks at the provider. Not recommended.

errors.actionFailed: Action failed. Check the provider credentials and try again.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Manage CoReview behavior across automation and knowledge capture.

automation.title: Automation
automation.description: Configure which commands run automatically on PRs and pushes, per git provider.

knowledgeBase.title: Knowledge Base
knowledgeBase.description: Control the /learn command, automatic rule-based capture, retrieval, and refinement.

knowledgeBase.title: Knowledge Base
knowledgeBase.description: Capture reviewer feedback as durable learnings and surface them during reviews.

master.title: Status
master.description: Turn the knowledge base off to pause both capture and retrieval. Existing data is preserved.
master.enabledHelp: When disabled, /learn is rejected and past learnings are not injected into reviews.

capture.title: Capture
capture.description: Pick how PR comments become learnings: the explicit /learn command or automatic rule matching.
capture.explicitLearnHelp: Reviewers capture learnings by typing /learn <text>. Recommended. Disable to fall back to rule-based extraction.
capture.commandHelp: The token reviewers type to capture a learning. Default is /learn.

rules.title: Automatic extraction rules
rules.activeHelp: Rules match each PR comment case-insensitively. A hit captures the entire comment as a learning. Active because the /learn command is disabled.
rules.disabledHelp: Rules only run when the /learn command is disabled. We keep your list here so the feature is ready if you ever toggle off.
rules.label: Rule phrases
rules.description: Any phrase below that appears in a comment triggers capture.

retrieval.title: Retrieval
retrieval.description: Control how past learnings are surfaced during reviews.
retrieval.applyToReviewHelp: When on, matching learnings are added to the context PR-Agent uses for /review.
retrieval.maxRetrievedHelp: Upper bound on how many learnings PR-Agent can attach to a single review.
retrieval.maxSummaryHelp: Truncate each learning's injected summary to this many characters.

refinement.title: Refinement
refinement.description: Stage 2 background worker settings. The worker picks up raw learnings and turns them into polished entries.
refinement.duplicateThresholdHelp: Cosine similarity above which two learnings are considered duplicates (0-1). Higher is stricter.

legacy.title: Legacy passive capture
legacy.description: Older @-mention based capture. Kept for backward compatibility; will be removed in a future release.
legacy.captureHelp: Scan PR comments that mention the bot for preference markers. Superseded by the /learn command and extraction rules above.
legacy.requireMentionHelp: Only considers comments that explicitly tag the bot. Ignored when the legacy capture is off.

messages.saved: Knowledge base config saved. Webhook processes will pick up changes on the next delivery.
messages.reloaded: Cache invalidated. Next webhook delivery will re-read the config from the database.

errors.loadFailed: Failed to load knowledge base config.
errors.saveFailed: Failed to save knowledge base config.
errors.reloadFailed: Failed to invalidate the config cache.
